description: Fix bugs, resource-leak patterns, audit gaps, and a logic error found during backend code audit of the MCP server package.

userStories.title: Fix removeLocalPlugin configWriteMutex pattern to match config.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the configWriteMutex pattern in removeLocalPlugin to be consistent with saveConfig/saveToolConfig so that concurrent config writes don't see a rejected mutex promise on error.
acceptanceCriteria: removeLocalPlugin uses the same mutex pattern as saveConfig: `state.configWriteMutex = writePromise.catch(() => {})` followed by `await writePromise`
The .catch() handler no longer re-throws, preventing the mutex from being temporarily assigned a rejected promise
Error logging is preserved (failures are still logged via log.warn)

userStories.title: Add audit entries for denied and disabled browser tool calls
userStories.description: As an operator monitoring the OpenTabs server, I want all browser tool call attempts (including denied and disabled ones) to appear in the audit log so that I have complete visibility into tool usage patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: When a browser tool call is blocked because the tool is disabled, an audit entry is appended with success=false and error.code='DISABLED'
When a browser tool call is denied by permission policy, an audit entry is appended with success=false and error.code='PERMISSION_DENIED'
When a browser tool confirmation times out, an audit entry is appended with success=false and error.code='CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT'
When a user denies a browser tool confirmation, an audit entry is appended with success=false and error.code='USER_DENIED'
Existing audit entries for successful/failed browser tool executions are unchanged
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts, function handleBrowserToolCall (lines 159-294).

Currently, audit entries are only appended in the `finally` block of the actual execution path (lines 284-293). Four early-return paths skip audit logging:

1. Tool disabled check (line 166-171): return before audit
2. Permission denied (line 192-201): return before audit
3. User denied confirmation (line 234-244): return before audit
4. Confirmation timeout (line 249-258): return before audit

For each early return, add an `appendAuditEntry(state, { ... })` call with appropriate error codes. Use `Date.now()` for the timestamp and `0` for durationMs (since no execution happened). The `plugin` field should be `'browser'` (consistent with line 288).

Import `appendAuditEntry` from `./state.js` — it is already imported on line 19.

userStories.title: Fix detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl to count distinct endpoints instead of call counts
userStories.description: As a user of the plugin_analyze_site tool, I want the primary API base URL detection to correctly identify prefixes shared by multiple distinct endpoints, not just prefixes with high call counts from a single endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: prefixCounts accumulates the number of distinct endpoints per prefix, not the sum of callCount values
totalEndpoints is set to endpoints.length (distinct endpoint count), not the sum of callCount
A single endpoint called many times does not inflate the prefix count beyond 1
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/detect-apis.ts, function detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl (lines 320-364).

Two changes needed:

1. Line 332 and 335: Change `+ endpoint.callCount` to `+ 1`. Each endpoint should contribute 1 to each prefix it matches, regardless of how many times it was called. Currently:
```ts
prefixCounts.set(parsed.origin, (prefixCounts.get(parsed.origin) ?? 0) + endpoint.callCount);
```
Should be:
```ts
prefixCounts.set(parsed.origin, (prefixCounts.get(parsed.origin) ?? 0) + 1);
```
Same change for the inner loop on line 335.

2. Line 345: Change from summing callCount to using endpoints.length:
```ts
const totalEndpoints = endpoints.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.callCount, 0);
```
Should be:
```ts
const totalEndpoints = endpoints.length;
```

The comment on line 344 says 'shared by at least 2 endpoints' which confirms the intent is distinct endpoints, not call counts.
